from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {obj} feet above sea level",
        # more natural phrasing without redundant unit words
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "ICAO location identifier is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "location identifier is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "IATA location identifier is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "its first runway has a {obj} surface",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "its first runway number is {obj}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "its second runway has a {obj} surface",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "its third runway has a {obj} surface",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "its fourth runway has a {obj} surface",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "its fifth runway has a {obj} surface",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "currency is {obj}",
        "owner": "owner is {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "county seat is {obj}",
        "mayor": "mayor is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "leader party is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {obj}",
        "chief": "chief is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "hub airport is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "native name is {obj}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "is of the class {obj}",
        "division": "is of the division {obj}",
        "order": "is of the order {obj}",
        "species": "is of the species {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym is {obj}",
    }

    # Helper to format numbers nicely (remove trailing .0 and add commas)
    def _fmt_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            num = float(val.replace(",", ""))
            if num.is_integer():
                num = int(num)
            return f"{num:,}"
        except Exception:
            return val

    # Helper to build a description for a subject, possibly chaining to related subjects
    def _describe(subject, visited=None):
        if visited is None:
            visited = set()
        if subject in visited:
            return ""
        visited.add(subject)

        tlist = subject_map.get(subject, [])
        # Prioritize location, country, language related predicates for chaining
        location_triple = next((t for t in tlist if t.predicate == "location"), None)
        country_triple = next((t for t in tlist if t.predicate == "country"), None)
        language_triple = next((t for t in tlist if t.predicate == "language"), None)
        official_lang_triple = next((t for t in tlist if t.predicate == "officialLanguage"), None)

        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            if tmpl:
                phrase = tmpl.format(obj=obj)
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                phrase = f"{pred} {obj}"
            parts.append((t.predicate, phrase, obj))

        # Build base sentence for the subject
        if not parts:
            return ""

        # If there is only one simple predicate, use specialized phrasing
        if len(parts) == 1:
            pred, phrase, raw_obj = parts[0]
            if pred == "runwayLength":
                return f"The runway length at {subject} is {raw_obj} meters."
            if pred == "1stRunwaySurfaceType":
                return f"The first runway at {subject} is made from {raw_obj}."
            if pred == "4thRunwaySurfaceType":
                return f"The fourth runway at {subject} has a surface of {raw_obj.lower()}."
            if pred in ("locationIdentifier", "icaoLocationIdentifier", "iataLocationIdentifier"):
                code_type = {"locationIdentifier":"location identifier","icaoLocationIdentifier":"ICAO code","iataLocationIdentifier":"IATA code"}[pred]
                return f"The {code_type} for {subject} is {raw_obj}."
            if pred == "capital":
                return f"The capital of {subject} is {raw_obj}."
            if pred == "language":
                lang = raw_obj
                if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                    lang = lang[:-len(" language")]
                return f"The language of {subject} is {lang}."
            if pred == "officialLanguage":
                lang = raw_obj
                if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                    lang = lang[:-len(" language")]
                return f"The official language of {subject} is {lang}."
            if pred == "demonym":
                return f"The people of {subject} are called {raw_obj}."
            if pred == "leader":
                return f"The leader of {subject} is {raw_obj}."
            if pred == "country":
                article = "the " if raw_obj.startswith("United") else ""
                return f"{subject} is in {article}{raw_obj}."
            # default
            return f"{subject} {phrase}."
        else:
            # Multiple predicates: join with commas and “and”
            phrase_list = [p for _, p, _ in parts]
            return f"{subject} " + ", ".join(phrase_list[:-1]) + ", and " + phrase_list[-1] + "."

    # Build sentences, chaining location → country → language when possible
    sentences = []
    processed = set()
    for subject in subject_map:
        if subject in processed:
            continue
        # Start with the main subject description
        main_desc = _describe(subject)
        # Check for location chaining
        loc = next((t for t in subject_map[subject] if t.predicate == "location"), None)
        if loc:
            loc_obj = loc.object.strip('"')
            if loc_obj in subject_map:
                # Build country clause
                country_desc = _describe(loc_obj)
                # If the location description already contains the location name, we replace it
                # Example: "Aarhus Airport is located in Tirstrup."
                # We want: "Aarhus Airport is located in Tirstrup, which is in Denmark ..."
                if "in " + loc_obj in main_desc:
                    # remove trailing period if any
                    base = main_desc.rstrip('.')
                    # add relative clause
                    # get country info from loc_obj description (which may include its own country)
                    # Extract country name if present
                    country_triple = next((t for t in subject_map[loc_obj] if t.predicate == "country"), None)
                    if country_triple:
                        country_name = country_triple.object.strip('"')
                        # language clause
                        language_triple = next((t for t in subject_map[loc_obj] if t.predicate == "language"), None)
                        if language_triple:
                            lang = language_triple.object.strip('"')
                            if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                                lang = lang[:-len(" language")]
                            clause = f", which is in {country_name} where the language is {lang}"
                        else:
                            clause = f", which is in {country_name}"
                        sentence = base + clause + "."
                    else:
                        # fallback to full description of location
                        sentence = base + ", which " + country_desc[0].lower() + country_desc[1:] + "."
                else:
                    sentence = main_desc
            else:
                sentence = main_desc
        else:
            sentence = main_desc

        sentences.append(sentence)
        processed.add(subject)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
